3.happening: Measure the grid — how many rows it has.
3.why: We need the bounds to scan every cell and test neighbors.
3.invariant: The grid's dimensions are fixed for the whole run.

4.happening: Measure how many columns each row has.
4.why: Column count bounds our left/right moves.
4.invariant: Every row has the same width.

5.why: We must remember which cells we've reached so an island isn't measured twice.

6.happening: Loop over each row to give it a fresh tracker row.
6.why: state must mirror the grid's shape.
6.invariant: Each built row holds exactly {cols} zeros.

7.happening: Add a row of zeros — every cell starts unseen (0).
7.invariant: No cell is marked before the search begins.

8.why: We haven't seen any island yet.
8.invariant: best is the largest island area found so far.

9.happening: Reset the current-row marker to -1 (no active cell).
9.why: There's an active cell only while flooding an island.

10.why: Same — no cell is being processed yet.
10.invariant: cur_r and cur_c together locate the active cell.

11.happening: List the four orthogonal moves (up, down, right, left).
11.why: Islands connect only horizontally or vertically, never diagonally.
11.invariant: Only these four directions are ever tried.

12.happening: Scan rows from top to bottom looking for land.
12.why: Every cell must be checked to find all islands.
12.invariant: Rows above sr are fully scanned.

13.happening: Scan columns left to right within row {sr}.
13.why: We inspect each cell exactly once.
13.invariant: Cells left of sc in this row are already handled.

14.happening: Cell ({sr},{sc}) is land we haven't reached — a new island begins here.
14.why: Any unvisited land cell must belong to a brand-new island.
14.invariant: Every island is measured from its first-scanned cell.

14.happening: Cell ({sr},{sc}) is water or already flooded — skip it.
14.why: Water starts no island, and flooded land is already measured.
14.invariant: We never measure the same island twice.

15.happening: Start this island's area count at 0.
15.why: We'll add one for each land cell we flood.
15.invariant: area counts cells in the current island only.

16.happening: Make an empty stack for this island's flood.
16.why: The stack holds discovered cells not yet explored.
16.invariant: Depth-first: we always explore the most recent cell next.

17.happening: Push the seed cell ({sr},{sc}) onto the stack.
17.invariant: The stack holds only this island's frontier.

18.happening: Mark the seed cell as seen (1).
18.why: So it can never be pushed again.
18.invariant: A cell enters the stack at most once.

19.happening: The stack holds {len(stack)} cell(s) still to explore — keep flooding.
19.why: Unexplored frontier remains in this island.
19.invariant: The stack empties as the island fills.

19.happening: The stack is empty — this island is completely flooded.
19.invariant: area now holds this island's full size.

20.happening: Pop the top cell off the stack.
20.why: Depth-first explores the most recently discovered cell first.
20.invariant: The popped cell is about to become the active cell.

21.why: We need its coordinates to reach neighbors.

22.why: Row and column together locate the active cell.
22.invariant: cur_r, cur_c now point at the cell being flooded.

23.happening: Mark the active cell ({cur_r},{cur_c}) fully done (2).
23.why: It now firmly belongs to the current island.

24.happening: Count this cell — the island's area grows to include it.
24.why: Area is the number of land cells in the island.
24.invariant: area equals the cells flooded in this island so far.

25.happening: Try each of the four directions from this cell.
25.why: To find land connected to it.
25.invariant: All four neighbors are examined before moving on.

26.why: The direction's row offset added to the active cell.
26.invariant: nr may be off-grid — we check next.

27.why: The direction's column offset added to the active cell.
27.invariant: nc may be off-grid — we check next.

28.when: nr >= 0 && nr < rows && nc >= 0 && nc < cols && grid[nr][nc] == 1 && state[nr][nc] == 0
28.happening: Neighbor ({nr},{nc}) is fresh land — add it to the frontier.
28.why: It's in-bounds, is land, and hasn't been seen.
28.invariant: Only new land ever joins the stack.

28.happening: Neighbor ({nr},{nc}) is off-grid, water, or already seen — ignore it.
28.why: Only in-bounds unseen land can extend the island.
28.invariant: Out-of-range and visited cells are never re-added.

29.happening: Mark the new land neighbor ({nr},{nc}) as seen (1).
29.why: Claim it before pushing so it can't be queued twice.
29.invariant: Seen cells are waiting in the stack.

30.happening: Push the neighbor onto the stack.
30.why: So we'll explore it and its own neighbors later.

31.happening: Island fully measured (area {area}) — keep it if it beats the best.
31.why: The answer is the maximum area over all islands.
31.invariant: best holds the largest island area found so far.

32.why: No active cell while we resume scanning.
32.invariant: cur_r back to -1 hides the cursor.

33.why: The cursor is fully hidden until the next island.
33.invariant: The next land cell found starts a new flood.

34.why: Every cell has been scanned and every island measured.

init.happening: Setting up: measuring the grid and preparing an empty visited-tracker.
init.why: We need dimensions and a way to remember reached cells before searching.

loop.happening: Scanning the grid for the next piece of unvisited land.
loop.why: Each unvisited land cell reveals a new island to measure.

check.happening: Deciding whether this cell starts or extends an island.
check.invariant: Every island is measured exactly once.

update.happening: Recording progress — counting a cell or updating the best area.
update.why: State must reflect what we've discovered.
update.invariant: A claimed cell stays claimed; best only grows.

move.happening: Advancing the flood — popping a cell and reaching toward neighbors.
move.why: Depth-first exploration walks the connected land.

return.happening: Search complete — reporting the largest area: {best}.
return.why: All cells scanned, all islands measured.
